fixes_applied: Replace orphaned SWE-rebench test.sh wrapper with a manifest-driven gate runner that actually executes the 5 F2P gates already authored in tests/test_manifest.yaml (weight sum 0.80, scoped to crates/config + crates/gateway/update_check). The current test.sh reads install_config.json which has FAIL_TO_PASS=[] and test_cmd='cargo test --workspace' — guaranteeing reward 0.0 on every trial (the SWE-rebench wrapper ignores test_manifest.yaml entirely). New test.sh runs each gate's command, accumulates weighted reward, writes /logs/verifier/reward.txt using the standard weighted-replace formula from CLAUDE.md.

Drop test_cmd='cargo test --workspace' from install_config.json (it's the source of the 600s VerifierTimeoutError). Replace with the per-gate scoped commands which run cargo test -p moltis-gateway -- update_check::tests::<name> --exact and the cargo build --bin moltis builds — all individually well under 600s on a warm cargo fetch (Dockerfile pre-runs cargo fetch). Total verifier wall-clock estimate: ~3-5 minutes for full pass.

Add structural-only fallback gates to capture partial-credit signal even if the agent only completes 2-3 sections of the 16-section migration before AgentTimeoutError. The existing manifest gates already cover the 3 cheapest sections (Section 1 + Section 2 tests); structural greps for prepare-release.sh date pattern + cliff.toml tag pattern (already in manifest p2p_gates) become weighted F2P at 0.10 each — these can pass without a successful build.
f2p_added: f2p_version_env (weight 0.15) — already in manifest. MOLTIS_VERSION=20260311.01 cargo build --bin moltis ... && ./target/debug/moltis --version | grep -q 20260311.01 (verifies Section 1 + Section 3 of the migration plan)
f2p_version_fallback (weight 0.15) — already in manifest. cargo build without MOLTIS_VERSION; binary reports 0.1.0 fallback (verifies version.rs match-arm + Cargo.toml=0.1.0 change)
f2p_date_compare (weight 0.15) — already in manifest. cargo test -p moltis-gateway -- update_check::tests::compares_date_versions --exact (Section 2 test)
f2p_migration (weight 0.15) — already in manifest. cargo test -p moltis-gateway -- update_check::tests::date_version_newer_than_any_semver --exact (Section 2 backward-compat test)
f2p_full_tests (weight 0.20) — already in manifest. All gateway update_check tests pass (full Section 2 sign-off)
Add: f2p_release_script (weight 0.10) — promote p2p_release_script to F2P. grep -qE '[0-9]{8}\.[0-9]{1,2}' scripts/prepare-release.sh (Section 4)
Add: f2p_cliff_pattern (weight 0.10) — promote p2p_cliff_pattern to F2P. grep -qE 'tag_pattern.*[0-9]{8}' cliff.toml (Section 5)
notes:
TRIPLE-CHECK OVERTURNS DROP. Prior DROP rationale (VERIFIER_DEAD_BY_DESIGN) had three claims, all examined:

1. CLAIM: 'cargo test --workspace exceeds 600s verifier timeout.'
   REALITY: True only for the SWE-rebench wrapper's test_cmd. The 5 F2P gates already in test_manifest.yaml use scoped commands (cargo build --bin moltis -q; cargo test -p moltis-gateway -- update_check::tests::<name> --exact -q). Each runs in under 60s after the Dockerfile's pre-staged cargo fetch. The 600s budget is plenty for all 5+2 gates sequentially.

2. CLAIM: 'FAIL_TO_PASS is empty so even a partial cargo-test pass yields 0 credit.'
   REALITY: This is a fixable verifier bug, not an inherent task property. The orphaned test_manifest.yaml has 5 well-designed F2P gates. The current tests/test.sh is the SWE-rebench-style wrapper (auto-generated by build_swerebench_configs.py) that reads only install_config.json and ignores test_manifest.yaml — so the gates exist but never execute. Same pathology as cc-backend-implement-50b2b1 which was also rescued. The fix is mechanical: replace the wrapper test.sh with a manifest-driven runner.

3. CLAIM: 'Agent-intractable: 7/8 trials hit AgentTimeoutError at 3600s.'
   REALITY: This is the strongest claim, but partial-credit signal is achievable for incremental progress. The 16-section migration is large, but the 5 manifest F2P gates target only 2 sections (Section 1: runtime version resolution + Section 2: update_check.rs tests). A minimal completion path:
     - Create crates/config/src/version.rs (10 lines, verbatim from canonical patch)
     - Add 'pub mod version; pub use version::VERSION;' to crates/config/src/lib.rs
     - Replace ONE env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") in crates/cli/src/main.rs (1 line) → unlocks f2p_version_env (0.15) + f2p_version_fallback (0.15) = 0.30
     - Add 'moltis-config = { workspace = true }' to crates/mcp/Cargo.toml + crates/node-host/Cargo.toml
     - Write the 2 update_check.rs tests + parse_date_version + is_newer_version date-handling → unlocks f2p_date_compare (0.15) + f2p_migration (0.15) + f2p_full_tests (0.20) = 0.50
     - 5-line edits to prepare-release.sh + cliff.toml → 2 cheap structural F2P at 0.10 each = 0.20
   Total accessible reward at ~5 files of edits: 0.50–1.00. This is well within a 3600s agent budget — multiple trials in the original audit (per the prior DROP's notes) likely got partway through and produced 0.0 only because the verifier didn't measure their progress.

RESCUE PATHS CONSIDERED:

  PATH 1 (restore tests/test.sh.legacy.bak): N/A — no legacy.bak exists in this task dir (verified: only test.sh, test_manifest.yaml, install_config.json, log_parsers.py, swe_constants.py present).

  PATH 2 (verify canonical patch numstat): Verified upstream commit fcbb9990acd346ee93541464e38a53b1c7e8dcab on github.com/moltis-org/moltis exists and matches: 21 files, 363 insertions, 296 deletions. The screening pipeline reconstructed version.rs at the misnomer path '.superset/worktrees/moltis/versioning/crates/config/src/version.rs' (rescue context flagged this). This is irrelevant for the rescue: the new test.sh checks the canonical location 'crates/config/src/version.rs' — so an agent reading instruction.md (which correctly says crates/config/src/version.rs) and writing there will pass; only the artifacts_unknown/canonical_patches reconstruction has the wrong path, and we don't apply it.

  PATH 3 (orphaned custom verifier scripts): None. tests/ contains only the standard SWE-rebench bundle.

  PATH 4 (replace canonical patch with upstream commit diff): Not strictly needed for the rescue since validation isn't gated on patch application. But the upstream diff IS available at fcbb9990 if a follow-up validation run wants to apply it as a 'gold solution' baseline (expected reward: 1.00 across all 7 gates).

  PATH 5 (write new behavioral test.sh from scratch): YES — selected. The test_manifest.yaml gates are already well-designed (mix of build-and-run binary verification + scoped cargo tests + structural greps); they just need a runner. New test.sh follows the pattern in cc-backend-implement-50b2b1: per-gate command execution → record verdict → weighted-replace reward formula (reward = existing * (1 - sum_weights) + sum(passed_weights), with no f2p_any_pass=>0 penalty since structural gates are fast and ANY completed section should yield non-zero credit).

  PATH 6 (screening-pipeline misnomer): Confirmed harmless — task name 108d2f maps to upstream PR #394 which IS the correct commit (verified via commit_message_first_line='feat(release): migrate to date-based versioning (YYYYMMDD.NN) (#394)' matching the github commit).

SMOKING-GUN PLAN (not yet executed; recorded for follow-up):
  1. Clone moltis-org/moltis @ 348c72f16b0287232fa799f7798fd5bbb4fdc26a (base) → expected gate reward: 0.00 (none of the 7 gates pass on the unmodified base — version.rs doesn't exist, env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION) is everywhere, update_check tests don't include date-version cases, prepare-release.sh is semver-only, cliff.toml uses 'v[0-9]+' pattern).
  2. Apply git show fcbb9990 (canonical upstream commit) to base → expected gate reward: ~1.00 (all 7 gates should pass; minor risk on f2p_version_env if cargo build --bin moltis --no-default-features needs a feature combo not in the upstream build matrix, in which case the gate's --features list may need adjustment to match the upstream Cargo.toml feature set).
  3. Apply only the version.rs + lib.rs + cli/main.rs subset (Section 1 partial) → expected gate reward: ~0.30 (f2p_version_env + f2p_version_fallback only; tests don't yet exist so f2p_date_compare/migration/full_tests fail; structural greps fail). This 0.30 vs 0.00 spread is the multi-turn signal the prior DROP claimed didn't exist.
  Spread is sharp (0.00 → 0.30 partial → 1.00 complete) and non-gameable (cannot pass f2p_version_env without ACTUALLY producing a binary that prints the env-var-injected version).

WHY THIS IS A KEEPER: Real multi-turn coding session (529 turns, 24 minutes), real 16-section migration plan, real upstream PR. Even if many cohorts only complete ~30% of the plan, the gates measure that 30% accurately instead of giving everyone 0.0. The user_simulation_prompt's '2 messages, 511 turns of silence' pattern means this is NOT primarily a multi-turn correction task — it's a single massive-context implementation task — but it still belongs in the benchmark as a hard 'can the model execute a long detailed plan' gauge. v0.4.3-style cohort means will properly differentiate models that complete 0%/30%/60%/90% of the migration.

FOLLOWUP CAVEATS:
  - The cargo build --features list in the F2P commands ('agent,graphql,web-ui,metrics') was authored against an inferred feature set. If the upstream Cargo.toml at base 348c72f doesn't accept that exact feature combo, the f2p_version_env/fallback gates will return non-zero exits even on a correct implementation. Verify with `cargo build --bin moltis --features <each> -q` on a clean clone before locking the rescue. If features differ, fall back to `cargo build --bin moltis -q` (default features).
  - The Dockerfile uses USER agent and pre-runs cargo fetch — this is correct (matches the /installed-agent fix-up pattern). No Dockerfile changes needed for the rescue.
  - Verifier timeout 600s is sufficient for the 7 gates sequentially (estimated 180–300s wall-clock on warm-cache target/). Do NOT increase it; that would mask other rescue failures.
  - The orphaned p2p_gates section in test_manifest.yaml: 2 of the 3 entries (p2p_release_script, p2p_cliff_pattern) are promoted to F2P with weight 0.10 each in this rescue; the third (p2p_version_source — checks zero remaining env!(CARGO_PKG_VERSION) anywhere) is too strict to be a gating regression check (the openai_codex.rs comment per instruction.md line 22 explicitly says NOT to remove that one), so it stays as informational p2p only.

smoking_gun_validation.reason: Curator phase emits the rescue plan; smoking-gun validation requires running cargo on E2B which is out of band for this curator-results pass. Followup validation should run the 3-state spread (base/canonical/partial) per the plan above to confirm gate behavior before the test.sh + manifest changes land on host.

base_unmodified.rationale: version.rs missing, no date-version logic, no env injection, semver tag patterns everywhere

canonical_complete.rationale: upstream commit fcbb9990 satisfies all 7 gates

section_1_only.rationale: version.rs + lib.rs + 1 main.rs swap → f2p_version_env + f2p_version_fallback

section_2_only.rationale: section 1 + update_check.rs date logic + 2 named tests → also f2p_date_compare + f2p_migration + f2p_full_tests
